database_meta.name: AI Wingman Forge — Public Platform Database
database_meta.description: Sanitized platform data for the Forge build planner.
database_meta.license: Public manufacturer specs, DoD program announcements, industry reporting.

compliance.note: First FPV on Blue UAS list. Sanctioned by China Dec 2025.

specs.radios: Custom in-house jam-resistant C2 and VTX
specs.manufacturing: El Segundo, CA — scaling to 10,000/month target

gcs.crossbow: Tactical GCS — EW resistance at range
gcs.flatbow: Soldier-borne — 3 drones per field pack, 150m displaced antenna, anti-jamming

platforms.documentation_availability: Minimal public — most specs locked. neros.tech
platforms.description: The Archer is a compact, expendable FPV strike drone designed for asymmetric warfare. First FPV platform ever approved for the Blue UAS list. Battle-tested in Ukraine (6,000+ units delivered via International Drone Coalition) before U.S. military evaluators touched it. Neros finished 2nd overall in the Drone Dominance Gauntlet Phase I (87.5 points out of 100).

compliance.note: DDG Phase I winner — procurement pathway, not Blue UAS

specs.ew_immunity: Complete — zero RF emissions, invisible to SIGINT, immune to jamming
specs.ai: Gunslinger AI for pilot decision support

other_products.gryphon: Heavy-lift hydrogen fuel cell multirotor, 2-3hr endurance, 200km range

platforms.documentation_availability: Gryphon spec sheet at skycutteruas.co.uk. Shrike specs limited.
platforms.description: The Shrike 10 Fiber won the Drone Dominance Gauntlet Phase I with 99.3 points — the highest score of all 26 competitors. It is a fiber-optic FPV strike drone, meaning the video and control signals travel through a thin fiber-optic cable rather than RF, making it completely immune to electronic warfare jamming.

specs.frame: CL2 3K carbon fiber, 8mm arm, 19mm motor mount
specs.motors: UMAC 2807 (28mm x 7mm stator, N552SH magnets, 12mm bearings, 3-8S)
specs.flight_controller: Brave F7 (STM32 F722, BMI270 Gyro, BMP390 Barometer)
specs.camera: FatShark Aura Analog (1200 TVL, Sony 1/3" CMOS)
specs.black_widow: Army SRR Tranche 2 Program of Record

black_widow.ai: FLIR Prism AI + Palantir VNav GPS-denied

platforms.documentation_availability: Specs at redcat.red/fang. Black Widow at redcat.red/black-widow.
platforms.description: The FANG (Fierce, Agile, Nimble, Guerrilla) F7 is Red Cat's entry into the tactical FPV strike market. Built with NDAA-compliant components including UMAC (Unusual Machines) American-made motors. Red Cat is also the manufacturer of the Teal 2 (ISR) and Teal Black Widow (Army SRR Program of Record).

specs.autopilot: VOXL 2 Mini (Qualcomm QRB5165, 8 cores @ 3.091 GHz, 8GB LPDDR5, 15+ TOPS AI)
specs.video_system: MVX — US-built, ChaCha256 encrypted, sub-40ms latency, dual-band digital HD, 11km+ tested
specs.controller: Zorro Blue (gutted Radiomaster Zorro, US ModalAI electronics, ELRS 915MHz)

specs.note: Blue UAS indoor FPV, VOXL 2 Mini

specs.navigation: GNSS-independent — inertial + visual + RF-based layered nav
specs.founders: Former Danish Jægerkorpet special forces operators

platforms.documentation_availability: Intentionally minimal. renegadeuxs.com provides no tech specs or detailed images.
Product page at pdw.ai/products/c100-defense. Available via GSA and Darley.

other_products.vulcan: Deployable mobile drone factory — auto-generates thousands of drones, 150+ printable designs

platforms.description: Vantage Robotics builds compact ISR platforms for defense and public safety. The Vesper and Trace are small, portable reconnaissance drones designed for squad-level deployment with encrypted communications and secure data handling.

compliance.note: First autonomous interceptor on Blue UAS list

specs.autonomy: Self-deploys upon radar alert to engage hostile drones
specs.radar: Echodyne EchoFlight airborne MESA + EchoShield ground radar

compliance.note: First tethered drone on both Blue and Green UAS lists

specs.army_program: VHA-H (Variable Height Antenna - Heavy) by PEO Soldier
specs.note: Tethered persistent, unlimited endurance while tethered

specs.ai: Hivemind — GPS-denied, comms-contested nav, swarming (4→8→16+ teams, doubling annually)

platforms.manufacturer_hq: Gilching, Germany; Moorpark, CA (135,000 sq ft US facility, June 2025)

specs.sensors: HD40-LV (720p, 10x optical zoom, LWIR 640x480, laser illuminator, 360° gimbal)

platforms.description: VTOL fixed-wing platform from Germany's leading tactical drone manufacturer. The Trinity Pro is a commercial mapping/survey platform while the Vector AI is the defense variant with AI-powered autonomous capabilities. Quantum Systems won a Bundeswehr (German military) contract and has been active in the Ukrainian conflict.

specs.warhead: Modular — fragmentation AND EFP (new anti-armor capability)
specs.guidance: GPS with Tap-to-Target on tablet FCU

block_2.ai: NVIDIA GPU, Automatic Target Recognition (AI/ML edge)

specs.note: New — fills gap between SB 300 and SB 600. Targets LASSO requirement.

specs.autonomy: Lattice AI/ML — autonomous tracking, waypoint nav, object classification
specs.gcs: Touchscreen — select target, standoff distance, engagement angle

other_products.altius_600: 27 lb, 2.54m wingspan, 440km range, 4+ hrs, tube-launched, swarming
other_products.altius_600m: Strike variant, 3-7 lb warhead, ~160 km range
other_products.altius_700m: ~65 lbs, 33-35 lb warhead (Hellfire-class), 160+ km range, 75 min. Largest loitering munition on market.

compliance.note: UltraBlue/MicroBlue avionics on Framework. Platforms not individually Blue UAS Cleared.

specs.ultrablue: Bespoke NDAA-compliant avionics stack — mission computer + flight controller, in commercial deployment since 2020

specs.ai: Onboard AI for GNSS-denied navigation, target recognition, multi-drone single-operator
AI object recognition, Minerva C2 swarm software

specs.note: AR5 specs, maritime ISR, UK Border Force
Released to service RN/RM. Missile-armed variant tested July 2025
First in class certified NATO STANAG 4703

specs.type: ISR quadcopter with neural-net GPS-denied navigation

specs.milestones: First UCAV BVR air-to-air kill (Nov 2025), first autonomous close formation (Dec 2025)

specs.note: Multi-day formation flight tests completed March 2026

specs.anka3: stealth flying-wing UCAV, 6500kg, internal weapons bay

specs.ai: AI facial recognition, autonomous target engagement
specs.note: UN-reported first autonomous lethal drone engagement (Libya)
AI autonomous indoor micro LM, room-clearing

specs.ai: XOS operating system, Mark & Fly AR/VR

specs.ai_upgrade: NVIDIA Jetson autonomous target-finding (MS series June 2025)
specs.note: One-way attack, mass-produced, combat-proven Ukraine/Middle East
Wing Loong II specs, exported to 10+ countries
Loyal wingman to J-20, operational PLAAF ~2021, naval variant for Type 076
Tactical VTOL ISR, Indian Army standard
UVision Hero series under EU/Rheinmetall license
Shark ISR + PD-2 tactical + Leleka-100 recon
Stealth flying-wing UCAV, internal weapons bay

compliance.note: Blue UAS Cleared List. Army SRR Tranche 1.

specs.gnss: GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou + ADS-B In receiver

ai_autonomy.note: Industry-leading visual-inertial odometry, 360° obstacle avoidance, NightSense zero-light navigation. Deeply integrated into own hardware — not standalone.

platforms.description: Skydio's flagship AI-autonomous drone. The X10 is the commercial/public safety variant; the X10D is the defense variant with hardened comms, MAVLink support, and RAS-A compliance. Both feature six navigation cameras, NVIDIA Jetson Orin GPU, and the industry's most capable obstacle avoidance. Best-in-class autonomy. Built in the USA.
gotchas: X10 (commercial) is locked to Skydio software. No MAVLink, no QGC, no Mission Planner. If you need open protocol access, you need the X10D — which costs more and requires government/defense procurement.

specs.note: Loyal wingman UCAV concept, in development
C-UAS company, not a drone platform — DroneGun/DroneSentry
First fully French indigenous loitering munition family

platforms.description: Group 2/3 modular fixed-wing UAS with 3D-printed airframe. Packs into a single Pelican case, man-portable, launch-ready in under 10 minutes. Open architecture supports ISR, EW, comms relay, and light strike missions. Swappable propulsion modules (pusher prop or micro turbojet). 3D printed in ~9 hours, fully assembled in 36 hours. OCTRA flight controller/mission computer controls vehicles 10-1000+ lbs.

specs.launch_method: catapult or rail, hand launch possible

platforms.documentation_availability: limited (defense-focused, some public specs from AUSA/JIFX demos)

platforms.description: Hand-launchable miniature precision-guided system (mPGS). Under 10 lbs, ruck-portable, launch-ready in under 30 seconds. Onboard ATR (Automatic Target Recognition) with autonomous terminal guidance. Designed for small-team organic fires and real-time ISR. Priced for mass employment.

specs.recovery_method: terminal impact (loitering munition) or belly landing

platforms.documentation_availability: minimal (specs from Medium blog posts and conference appearances)

platforms.description: Group 1 FPV quadcopter combining Orqa's battle-tested technology with Firestorm's additive manufacturing. US-built with NDAA-compliant components. Partnership between Firestorm Labs and Orqa FPV for at-scale production. Designed for US warfighter and allied forces.

Open-architecture ISR quadcopter with NVIDIA Jetson Xavier NX/Orin NX onboard. GNSS-denied capable via VIO and optical flow. Foldable arms for backpack transport, deployable in 4 minutes. Supports Doodle Labs or Silvus datalinks. STANAG 4609 compliant video. Multiple payload options including FLIR Hadron 640R, NextVision Dragoneye 2/Starling, SIYI ZR10. Water resistant IP54.

specs.compute: NVIDIA Xavier NX 16GB or Orin NX 16GB
specs.payload_options: FLIR Hadron 640R, NextVision Dragoneye 2, NextVision Starling, SIYI ZR10

platforms.gcs: SteamDeck (default), compatible with most MAVLink GCS

platforms.description: NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano-powered FPV drone with AI-driven terminal guidance. Open architecture enables autonomous target acquisition and terminal guidance against armored vehicles with manual abort/takeover capability. 10-inch frame with 3kg payload capacity. Compatible with standard headsets (e.g. Radiomaster) and digital radios (Silvus, Doodle Labs). Single integrated PCB serves as flight controller and airframe top cover. Designed to overcome EW with autonomous guidance.

specs.ai_capability: autonomous target acquisition, terminal guidance, ATR

platforms.platform_name: Custom ArduPilot / PX4 Builds — General Integration Reference

specs.vehicle_support: Widest (copter, plane, heli, rover, sub, boat)
specs.configuration: More parameters, more flexibility, steeper curve

platforms.description: Any drone built on ArduPilot or PX4 firmware running Pixhawk-compatible hardware. This covers hundreds of commercial products (Freefly Astro, Inspired Flight IF1200, Watts Innovations Prism, Harris Aerial H6, CubePilot-based builds) and thousands of custom builds. The open-source ecosystem is the backbone of non-DJI drone integration.

platforms.platform_name: Holybro X500 V2 + Pixhawk 6X (PX4 Dev Kit)

specs.flight_controller: Pixhawk 6X (STM32H753 H7, triple IMU, Ethernet) or Pixhawk 6C
specs.telemetry: SiK radio (915 MHz or 433 MHz), plug-and-play
specs.interfaces: Multiple UART, I2C, SPI, CAN, ADC, Ethernet
specs.temperature_control: On-board heating for IMU temp stability

platforms.description: The reference development platform for PX4 and ArduPilot. Carbon fiber frame with no-solder assembly. Ships with Pixhawk 6X (or 6C), M10 GPS, and SiK telemetry radio. Mounts for Raspberry Pi, Jetson Nano, and Intel RealSense depth cameras.
gotchas: 18-minute flight time is short. Bigger batteries help but add weight. Budget for multiple batteries.
No obstacle avoidance out of the box. Add Intel RealSense + PX4 avoidance module if needed.
No weatherproofing. This is a fair-weather development platform.
SiK radios are low-bandwidth (~64 kbps). Fine for MAVLink telemetry, not for video.
Pixhawk 6X PM02D-HV power module requires firmware setting (`SENS_EN_INA228`) and ArduPilot 4.4+.
M10 GPS needs PX4 1.14+ or ArduPilot 4.3+. Older firmware won't see it.
Chinese-sourced components — not NDAA compliant. For federal work, look at ARK Electronics FC + U.S.-sourced components.

specs.rtk: Via D-RTK 2 or network RTK module

platforms.description: DJI's compact enterprise platform. Available in Thermal (Mavic 3T) and Multispectral (Mavic 3M) variants. The go-to portable enterprise drone for inspection, search & rescue, and precision agriculture. Lightweight, foldable, excellent camera. The platform most fire departments and small agencies buy first.
gotchas: NOT NDAA compliant.
No payload swap — fixed sensor package per variant.
Not as robust as M30T or M350 — smaller platform, less wind resistance.
Widely deployed — good parts availability and support network.

platforms.description: DJI's flagship prosumer/light commercial drone. The Mavic 4 Pro continues the Mavic line's focus on imaging quality in a foldable, portable form factor. Positioned below the Matrice 4 series for enterprise use but increasingly used in commercial operations where the full enterprise feature set isn't required.

specs.flight_time: 49 min (standard props, no wind, no payload)
specs.video_transmission: DJI O4 Enterprise, 20 MB/s, 130 ms latency

platforms.description: DJI's compact flagship enterprise drone series, launched January 2025. Two models: the Matrice 4T (thermal/inspection) and Matrice 4E (mapping/survey). Features onboard AI computing, laser rangefinder, and DJI O4 Enterprise transmission. Replaces the Mavic 3 Enterprise line for most enterprise use cases while complementing the larger M350 RTK for heavy-lift missions.

compliance.note: Autel claims NDAA compliance; XE variant targeting formal listing

platforms.description: Dual-sensor enterprise workhorse. The EVO II Enterprise V3 offers thermal + visible imaging in a portable platform with robust flight performance. Autel positions itself as the alternative to DJI for operators who need non-DJI solutions but don't require full Blue UAS verification.

Fixed-wing mapping drone designed for large-area survey and photogrammetry. Hand-launched, autonomous flight with RTK/PPK positioning. The eBee X covers up to 500 hectares in a single flight, making it the reference platform for agricultural, mining, and environmental survey applications.

specs.obstacle_avoidance: Front/rear phased array radar + binocular vision
specs.delivery_modes: Cargo mode (direct), Winch mode (30 m cable)

platforms.description: DJI's heavy-lift delivery drone. 4-axis, 8-propeller configuration. 30 kg payload (dual battery) / 40 kg (single battery). Cargo mode and winch mode (30 m retractable cable). FAA-approved with Remote ID in the US. Built for logistics, agriculture supply, emergency response, and industrial material transport.
gotchas: NOT NDAA compliant.
Winch kit sold separately — base price does not include it.
Battery and charger sold separately.
Requires careful weight/CG management — drone auto-detects payload weight and center of gravity.
FlyCart 100 (80 kg payload) exists but is China-only as of 2026.

specs.video_tx: DJI O3 Enterprise, 2.4/5.8 GHz, 15 km range

platforms.description: DJI's mid-tier enterprise drone — smaller and more portable than the M350 but still a serious inspection and public safety platform. Integrated dual-camera + thermal + laser rangefinder payload. Weather-sealed and designed for rapid deployment.
gotchas: Not NDAA compliant.
No payload flexibility — the integrated sensor package is the only option. No LiDAR, no custom payloads.
DJI Dock integration is powerful but locks you into DJI's cloud ecosystem.
If you need LiDAR or custom sensors, use the M350 RTK instead.

compliance.note: XE variant is NDAA compliant; standard V2 has mixed sourcing

platforms.description: Autel's flagship enterprise drone and the most direct competitor to the DJI M30T. Quad-sensor gimbal (wide, zoom, thermal, laser rangefinder), GPS-denied flight via SLAM, A-Mesh drone-to-drone networking, and **no geofencing**. Positioned as the go-to DJI alternative for public safety and inspection operators.
gotchas: No geofencing — good for operators who need flexibility, but means no built-in airspace safety net. Know your airspace.

specs.flow_rate: Up to 16 L/min (2 nozzles), 24 L/min (4 nozzles)
specs.obstacle_avoidance: 360° — front/rear phased array radar + binocular vision

platforms.description: DJI's flagship agricultural spraying/spreading drone. Coaxial twin-rotor design carrying 40 kg spraying or 50 kg spreading payloads. Dual atomizing spraying system, front/rear phased array radars, binocular vision. The dominant platform in commercial ag spraying worldwide. Covers ~52 acres/hour at 2 GPA.
gotchas: NOT NDAA compliant. Federal ag funding that requires compliant equipment cannot be used for this platform.
Requires Part 137 exemption for commercial agricultural application in the US.
88 lb empty weight — not remotely portable. Requires truck/trailer setup.
DJI FlyCart 100 and T60 exist in China but are NOT FAA approved in the US. The T50 is currently the largest DJI ag drone available in the US market.
Battery logistics are critical. 9-min fast charge with DJI C10000 charger + generator is the only way to maintain production rates.
Nozzle clogging with powder-base products was an issue on earlier models — dual atomizing centrifugal nozzles on T50 address this.

platforms.description: XAG's flagship agricultural drone family. The P100 Pro carries 50 kg payload with 60L spray tank, covering 70 acres/hour. The P150 steps up to 70L tank and 80 kg max payload (P150 Max). Modular hot-swap payload system: spraying, spreading, mapping, and cargo transport. Fully autonomous via XAG One App with RTK cm-level positioning. Biggest direct competitor to DJI Agras T50.
gotchas: NOT NDAA compliant. Chinese manufacturer — same procurement restrictions as DJI.
Massive platform — requires truck/trailer infrastructure, generator for fast charging.
Part 137 exemption required for commercial ag application in the US.
XAG ecosystem is less mature in North America than DJI Agras — fewer dealers, less local support.
Competes directly with DJI Agras T50 — DJI has better software integration, XAG has larger payload options.

compliance.note: NDAA compliant (designed and manufactured in USA)

specs.gps: Dual GPS, RTK-compatible (base station sold separately)
specs.controller: GroundLink: 13.3" Windows tablet + 2.4 GHz radio, NDAA compliant
specs.multi-drone: Up to 3 UAS from single ground station
specs.software: AgroSol GCS (lifetime access, no recurring fees)
specs.ndaa: Compliant — designed and manufactured in Houston, TX

platforms.description: American-made agricultural spray drone. 18-gallon capacity, 8-rotor configuration, up to 50 acres/hour at 2 GPA. NDAA-compliant GroundLink Controller. The largest and most capable US-made ag spray drone currently available. Designed for producers and applicators who need NDAA compliance or want to avoid Chinese supply chain dependencies.
gotchas: Requires Part 137 exemption — Hylio assists with onboarding (complimentary).
RTK base station sold separately. Budget for it.
30-minute battery charge time is slower than DJI's 9-minute fast charge — need more battery sets for production rates.
US-made means higher price point than Chinese alternatives (DJI, XAG).
AgroSol software is functional but not as polished as DJI Agras app.

specs.navigation: FlyAware™ — computer vision + LiDAR + NVIDIA GPU
specs.payloads_(modular): LiDAR surveying, UT (ultrasonic testing), radiation detection, gas sensing

platforms.description: The only collision-tolerant inspection drone in mass production. Designed for confined spaces — tanks, boilers, pressure vessels, tunnels, mines, ore passes. Protective carbon cage allows contact with walls and structures without crashing. LiDAR SLAM for GPS-denied navigation and 3D mapping. Unique reversing motors recover from upside-down flips.
gotchas: 12-minute flight time is limiting for large assets. High-capacity battery helps (17 min). Tether power unit eliminates limit entirely.
Not for outdoor use — the cage creates drag, no GPS, limited speed (7 m/s max).
Not NDAA listed — may be an issue for US federal confined-space inspection contracts.
Environments must be cleaned and inerted before flying in explosive atmospheres — drone is NOT ATEX certified.
$50K+ system cost — justified by the 90% cost reduction vs scaffolding/rope access, but significant upfront investment.

compliance.note: NOT compliant — banned from U.S. federal procurement as of Dec 2025 (American Security Drone Act)

platforms.description: The DJI M350 RTK is DJI's flagship enterprise platform and the successor to the M300 RTK. It is the most widely deployed commercial drone for inspection, mapping, and public safety worldwide. Closed ecosystem, but deep third-party payload support via E-Port and Payload SDK.
gotchas: NOT NDAA compliant. Banned from U.S. federal procurement as of December 2025 under the American Security Drone Act. If your funding source is federal, you cannot buy this.

platforms.description: The Black Widow is the U.S. Army's Short Range Reconnaissance (SRR) Tranche 2 Program of Record winner. Designed as a soldier-carried ISR platform for squad-level operations. Part of the Red Cat Holdings portfolio alongside the Teal 2 and FANG F7.

Precision inspection and mapping platform designed for infrastructure and industrial applications. NDAA compliant. Skyfish focuses on high-accuracy 3D modeling and measurement for sectors like energy, construction, and telecommunications.

specs.power: 6S LiPO or LiON, XT60 bulkhead connector
specs.materials: Carbon fiber, flexible FDM print (Shore 95A), stainless hardware

platforms.description: UAS Nexus Platform One is a modular, MOSA-first FPV platform designed to fuse Western drone technologies into a single interoperable ecosystem. Inspired by Ukraine's decentralized FPV production model, it supports multiple configurations from FPV training to autonomous mesh networking. The airframe accepts 7-inch and 10-inch propellers and is compatible with flight controllers, companion computers, and radios from across the Blue UAS supply chain. Solder-free option available for the trainer con
gotchas: Members-only store — Syndicate access requires membership request approval.
No fixed BOM — Platform One is an ecosystem, not a single SKU. Component selection varies by configuration and availability.
Decentralized manufacturing — high volume capacity but supply chain coordination is on the operator/integrator.
Multi-firmware support means no single "golden path" — operator must choose and configure autopilot stack.
Deeper product scrape pending — Syndicate catalog details are behind the membership wall.

compliance.note: Blue UAS Framework (16 components listed — most of any manufacturer)

platforms.description: ModalAI produces the most Blue UAS Framework components of any single manufacturer (16 listings). Their VOXL 2 compute platform is the reference companion computer for PX4-based autonomous drones. The Seeker and Stinger are ModalAI's own FPV/autonomous drone platforms built on this ecosystem.

specs.deployment: Two-person team, fits in rifle case, minutes to assemble

platforms.description: Anduril's medium-range reconnaissance drone designed for company-level military operations. Helicopter-type design (main + tail rotor) for superior endurance and payload compared to typical quadcopters. Controlled through Anduril's Lattice AI software. Combat-proven in Ukraine. Deployed at JPMRC Hawaii, Combined Resolve Germany, and Balikatan Philippines.
gotchas: Military/government procurement only — not available for commercial purchase.
Lattice is proprietary — no MAVLink, no open protocol access.
Helicopter design provides endurance but is mechanically more complex than multirotor.
Requires Anduril training and support pipeline.
Combat-deployed in Ukraine since 2022 — real-world validation, but also means adversaries have had time to study it.

platforms.description: SiFly entered the market with the Q12, a platform that prioritizes endurance and range above everything else. American-made, NDAA-compliant. Positioned for long-duration inspection and surveillance missions where flight time is the primary constraint.

specs.sensors: Stereo cameras, IMU, LiDAR (varies by config)

platforms.description: Indoor-capable autonomous quadcopter designed for GPS-denied, comms-denied operations. Runs Shield AI's Hivemind autonomy stack — the same AI pilot that has been integrated onto platforms from small quadcopters to the Anduril YFQ-44A CCA and GA-ASI MQ-20 Avenger. Nova 2 can explore buildings, map interiors, and provide ISR without GPS, communications, or human piloting.

Tethered drone system designed for persistent surveillance and communications relay. The tether provides continuous power and a hard-wired data link, eliminating battery endurance constraints and RF interception concerns. Designed for fixed-site security, event overwatch, and forward operating base (FOB) protection.

compliance.note: Blue UAS listed, Green UAS certified

specs.coverage: Up to 1,300 acres (~550 ha) per 1-hour flight
specs.cameras: Sony A7R IV (61MP), Sony RX1R II (42MP), MicaSense RedEdge-P
specs.capture_speed: ~10x faster than multicopters, ~30x faster than terrestrial
specs.payload_bay: Hot-swap, 6 dedicated payloads (RGB, oblique, LiDAR, multispectral)

platforms.description: Professional fixed-wing VTOL mapping drone. Vertical takeoff, fixed-wing cruise at 16 m/s for up to 59 minutes. PPK GNSS for cm-level accuracy without ground control points. Swappable sensors (61MP RGB, 42MP full-frame, multispectral, LiDAR). Over 100,000 flights worldwide. The WingtraRAY (2025) successor covers up to 1,300 acres in one hour-long flight.
gotchas: Fixed-wing means no hover capability during mission. Can't stop and inspect a point.
Requires open launch/landing area — tail-sitter VTOL needs clear space.
Not for inspection or close-proximity work — this is a mapping/survey platform only.
Proprietary autopilot — no MAVLink, no QGC, no Mission Planner.
Wind limits on ground (8 m/s for launch/land) are lower than in-flight limits.

compliance.note: Blue UAS certified, NDAA compliant, Green UAS certified

specs.max_payload: 19.1 lb (8.66 kg) under Part 107; 28.7 lb with certification

platforms.description: Heavy-lift hexacopter developed to U.S. Air Force standards. The workhorse of the NDAA-compliant drone world. Carries large payloads (LiDAR, EO/IR, Phase One cameras) with 43-minute endurance. Open payload interface. ArduPilot-based. SAFE motor-out redundancy keeps flying if a motor fails.
gotchas: Heavy platform — not backpackable. This is a truck drone, not a backpack drone.
Folding arms can be stiff after flight — thrust loads the locking mechanism. Support the arm bottom with upward force while sliding the lock.
Requires Part 107 waiver if total weight (aircraft + payload) exceeds 55 lb.
Batteries are proprietary.
No integrated camera — payload must be purchased separately. Budget accordingly.
SAFE motor-out capability is a lifesaver for expensive payloads but requires pilot awareness — land promptly when alerted.

platforms.description: One of the lightest enterprise drones on the market at 500g. French-designed. Triple-sensor payload (32x zoom, wide, FLIR thermal). GOV and MIL variants. The MIL version adds a Microhard radio for extended comms. ISO9001 certified manufacturing.
gotchas: Wi-Fi link on GOV version is range-limited and susceptible to interference in dense RF environments.
Small thermal sensor — FLIR Boson 320 vs competitors at 640×512. Adequate for detection, not ideal for detailed thermal analysis.
No payload swap. The integrated sensor package is all you get.
Lightweight is a double-edged sword — extremely portable, but poor wind resistance.
Parrot's enterprise support has historically been inconsistent compared to DJI/Autel/Skydio.

platforms.description: Coaxial drone platform — two counter-rotating rotors on a single axis. Compact, robust, and high-performance. Designed for industrial, defense, and public safety markets. The coaxial design provides exceptional stability and efficiency in a smaller footprint than equivalent multirotor platforms.

specs.max_flight_time: 25 min with LR1 payload, 30 min with 2 lb payload
specs.gnss: Multi-band with RTK (NTRIP or base station)
specs.connectivity: LTE via AuterionOS, Doodle Labs RF link

platforms.description: American-made, open-architecture mapping and inspection drone built on PX4. Running Auterion Enterprise PX4 on a Skynode flight controller with triple-redundant IMUs. Blue UAS certified. Swappable payload via Smart Dovetail system. The Astro Max variant carries the 61MP Sony LR1 full-frame sensor. Over 100,000 commercial flights logged.
gotchas: 25-minute flight time is shorter than competitors. DJI M350 does 55 min. Inspired Flight IF1200A does 43 min. If endurance is critical, factor this in.

platforms.description: Compact, rucksack-portable ISR drone optimized for night operations. "Dominate the Night." FLIR Hadron 640R EO/IR payload delivers exceptional thermal imaging in a Group 1 package. Used by DoD, law enforcement, and public safety.
gotchas: Small payload capacity — this is an ISR scout, not a mapping or inspection platform.
No swappable primary sensor beyond the Hadron EO/IR.
Near-silent propulsion is a tactical advantage but limits max speed and wind resistance.
Multi-vehicle command supported from single TAC.
